scenarios.opening_query: My rideshare account got deactivated over something on my driving record that shouldn't even be there.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal) || Criminal Law > Expunction
scenarios.hidden_fact: The record shows a dismissed DUII from six years ago — the arrest itself needs to be sealed, which is a court petition, not a DMV correction.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It shows a dismissed DUII from six years back — the arrest needs court sealing, not a DMV fix.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the record entry requires judicial sealing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The entry is someone else's accident merged onto my record — a pure DMV records-correction problem.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's someone else's accident merged onto my record — a DMV correction problem.

scenarios.opening_query: I got my third ticket this year in the same speed trap and I'm convinced it's rigged.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Traffic Offenses || Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The third ticket triggered the habitual-violation suspension — the DMV suspension hearing is now the fight that matters, not the ticket itself.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The third one triggered the habitual-violation suspension — the DMV hearing is the fight now, not the ticket.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an accumulation suspension has been triggered
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No suspension — I just want this ticket contested because their radar placement violates the calibration rules.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No suspension — I want the ticket fought over their radar calibration.
scenarios.vetting_note: Accumulation-trigger converts a ticket fight into an administrative hearing.

scenarios.opening_query: The interlock company reported me for 'violations' that were mouthwash and a dead battery.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > DMV (DUII Related) || Criminal Law > Parole/Probation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The interlock is a probation condition — the report went to my PO, and a probation-violation hearing is being scheduled; that's the venue that can jail me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The interlock is a probation condition — the report went to my PO and a violation hearing is coming. That's the venue that can jail me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the device reports feed probation rather than DMV
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No probation — the interlock is the DMV's reinstatement condition, and the false reports threaten my license only.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No probation — it's the DMV's reinstatement condition, so only my license is at risk.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same false-positive facts; supervising institution routes.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm a nurse and got a DUII last month. The criminal side has a lawyer but I'm panicking about everything else.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > DUII/DWS || Administrative Law > Professional Licensing
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'everything else' is my nursing license — the board requires self-reporting within 10 days and I haven't done it; that's the counsel I need now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my nursing license — the board requires self-reporting within 10 days and I haven't. That's the help I need.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether professional-license reporting duties are the live need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My license reporting is handled — what's left is the criminal case itself, and my current lawyer just withdrew.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: License reporting's handled — but my defense lawyer just withdrew, so it's the criminal case again.
scenarios.vetting_note: Collateral-consequence routing; self-report deadline is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: They suspended my license over a crash report that says I fled, which is completely untrue.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal) || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: A warrant issued yesterday — felony hit-and-run based on that report; the criminal case now leads everything.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A warrant issued yesterday — felony hit-and-run off that report. The criminal case leads now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether charges have issued on the disputed report
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The DA declined — the false report lives on only in the DMV suspension, which I need reversed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The DA declined — it's just the DMV suspension built on the false report.
scenarios.vetting_note: Charge-issuance fact between the parallel tracks.

scenarios.opening_query: My elderly father's license was taken after his fender bender and he's devastated.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Traffic Offenses || Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: No citation was even issued — the officer filed a medical-fitness referral, and it's the DMV's re-examination process he has to pass.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: No citation — the officer filed a medical-fitness referral, so it's the DMV re-exam process.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the action is a citation or a fitness referral
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He was cited for careless driving — contesting that citation is what protects the license.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He got a careless-driving citation — fighting that protects the license.
scenarios.vetting_note: Referral-vs-citation is the exact taxonomy boundary for elderly drivers.
